content_markdown: For a body to be in both translational and rotational equilibrium

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: it must not be in contact with any other objects.

options.label: B
options.text: the net torque and the net force acting on it must be zero.

options.label: C
options.text: the net torque acting on it must be zero.

options.label: D
options.text: the net force acting on it must be zero.

vibe_explanation: Translational equilibrium requires the vector sum of all external forces to be zero ($\sum \vec{F} = 0$), ensuring no linear acceleration. Rotational equilibrium requires the vector sum of all external torques to be zero ($\sum \vec{\tau} = 0$), ensuring no angular acceleration. For a body to be in both, both conditions must be satisfied simultaneously.
